assumptions: Threads are represented as a plain cylinder at major diameter; no helical geometry.
Head chamfer angle assumed 20 degrees (typical range 15-30).
code:
import cadquery as cq

# Parameters
across_flats = PARAMETERS['across_flats']
head_thickness = PARAMETERS['head_thickness']
washer_face_diameter = PARAMETERS['washer_face_diameter']
washer_face_thickness = PARAMETERS['washer_face_thickness']
nominal_diameter = PARAMETERS['nominal_diameter']
total_length = PARAMETERS['total_length']
thread_length = PARAMETERS['thread_length']
head_chamfer_angle = PARAMETERS['head_chamfer_angle']
end_chamfer_length = PARAMETERS['end_chamfer_length']

# Hex head
hex_head = (cq.Workplane("XY")
            .polygon(6, across_flats, circumscribed=True)
            .extrude(head_thickness))

# Washer face
washer_face = (cq.Workplane("XY")
               .circle(washer_face_diameter / 2)
               .extrude(washer_face_thickness))

# Combine head and washer face
head_assembly = hex_head.union(washer_face.translate((0, 0, -washer_face_thickness)))

# Bolt shank
shank_length = total_length - head_thickness - washer_face_thickness
bolt_shank = (cq.Workplane("XY")
              .circle(nominal_diameter / 2)
              .extrude(shank_length))

# Position shank at bottom of head assembly
bolt = head_assembly.union(bolt_shank.translate((0, 0, -shank_length)))

# Thread zone (plain cylinder, no threads)
# Already included in shank, no modification needed.

# Head chamfer: chamfer top edge of hex head
# Select top face of hex head and chamfer its edges
head_chamfer = (bolt.faces(">Z").workplane()
                .chamfer(head_chamfer_angle, head_thickness * 0.1))
# Note: chamfer on top face edges; simplified approach
# Actually, we need to chamfer the top edges of the hex head.
# Use edges of the top face.
bolt = bolt.faces(">Z").edges().chamfer(head_thickness * 0.1)

# End chamfer: chamfer bottom edge of shank
bolt = bolt.faces("<Z").edges().chamfer(end_chamfer_length)

result = bolt

operations.assumption: Threads are represented as a plain cylinder at major diameter; no helical geometry.

feature_summary.description: Hexagonal head extruded from a hexagon with across-flats dimension.
feature_summary.name: Hexagonal Head

feature_summary.description: Cylindrical washer face under the head.
feature_summary.name: Washer Face

feature_summary.description: Threaded portion of the shank, represented as plain cylinder.
feature_summary.name: Threaded Section

feature_summary.description: Chamfer on the top edge of the hexagonal head.
feature_summary.name: Head Top Chamfer

feature_summary.description: Chamfer at the free end of the shank.
feature_summary.name: Shank End Chamfer
